title: Glandularia tenera

The genus Glandularia is further classified within the order Lamiales. The classification of Glandularia tenera is also reflected in its botanical name, which indicates its scientific identity. intro: Glandularia tenera, also known as Verbena × hybrida, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Verbenaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as reducing inflammation, antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, and exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. The bioactive constituents of Glandularia tenera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Glandularia tenera is commonly used in traditional medicine and herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and fever.
